extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout outsprinted PIDCOCK Tom and SCHACHMANN Max from a 3-man break formed when Pidcock attacked after catching Kwiatkowski's solo move.
tactical_patterns: INEOS deployed 3-man squad (Kwiatkowski, Pidcock, Schachmann) into elite group, yet still lost to van Aert's superior sprint.
Crash at 53km triggered race selection before final circuits, forcing Bora-Hansgrohe and Israel into desperate chase mode.
PIDCOCK attacked immediately after catching KWIATKOWSKI's solo move, using fresh legs advantage to bridge with SCHACHMANN and van Aert.

rider_signals.PIDCOCK Tom: positive: attacking immediately after catching Kwiatkowski showed tactical maturity, nearly won sprint despite van Aert's presence
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: mechanical on final Cauberg ascent eliminated him from contention in closing kilometers
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: attacked on Cauberg but faded badly afterward, suggesting poor form or overextension
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: neutral: solo move caught but enabled Pidcock's counter-attack with INEOS numbers
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: crossed to elite group as last rider at age 40, finished 5th showing exceptional durability
